reasoning: The hypotheses encode absolute convergence of both series (partial sums of |a i| and |b i| converge). The expression ∑ j ∈ range (i+1), a j * b (i-j) is exactly the Cauchy product c_i = Σ_{j=0}^{i} a_j · b_{i-j}. The conclusion asserts that the partial sums of |c_i| converge, which is the standard definition of absolute convergence of the Cauchy product series. Using ℝ as the ambient type is a natural reading of Rudin Exercise 3.13 (Chapter 3, numerical series), and no extraneous hypotheses are added nor required ones dropped. The quantifier structure and mathematical content match the informal claim.
